_doc: LinkedIn PDF document-carousel slide content per article. make_carousel.py renders these to a multi-page PDF; buffer_carousel_runner posts to LinkedIn. Strategist adds new ones per CHANNEL_CONTENT_PLAYBOOK.md.

is-angi-worth-it.title: Is Angi Worth It for Contractors?

slides.headline: Is Angi worth it for contractors?
slides.body: The honest math most lead sites won't show you.

slides.headline: They sell the SAME lead to 5–12 of you
slides.body: Your $35 lead is really $35 × the 8 other trucks racing you to the phone.

slides.headline: Real cost per BOOKED job: $1,430–$2,500
slides.body: Once you factor the low close rates on shared leads. A job won organically? ~$290.

slides.headline: You're renting, not building
slides.body: Stop paying and the leads vanish. You bought at-bats, not an asset.

slides.headline: What to do instead
slides.body: Own your Google presence, systematize referrals, and capture the homeowners already on your site.

slides.headline: Run your own number.
slides.body: Free calculator → booked-job.com/blog/is-angi-worth-it

slides.headline: How to price a job without leaving money on the table
slides.body: The math nobody teaches you in the apprenticeship.

slides.headline: Markup is not margin
slides.body: A 50% markup is only a 33% margin. That gap is on every job you quote, all year.

slides.headline: Know your 3 numbers
slides.body: Direct cost, overhead, and profit. You can't price what you can't see.

slides.headline: The one formula
slides.body: Price = cost ÷ (1 − target margin). A $400 job at 55% margin = $889.

slides.headline: Stop racing to the bottom
slides.body: Win the customer before it's a bidding war — then you price for margin, not survival.

slides.headline: What should you charge?
slides.body: Free margin calculator → booked-job.com/blog/how-to-price-a-job

get-more-google-reviews.title: How to Get More Google Reviews

slides.headline: How to get more Google reviews
slides.body: The cheapest marketing most shops ignore.

slides.headline: Timing beats everything
slides.body: Ask at peak gratitude — the moment the job's done and the customer's happy.

slides.headline: Make it one tap
slides.body: Text the direct review link within the hour. Don't make them search for you.

slides.headline: Ask everyone
slides.body: Only asking happy customers is 'gating' — against Google's rules. And never pay for reviews.

slides.headline: Make it a habit
slides.body: Add the ask to the end of every job + your invoice. Steady trickle beats a one-time burst.

slides.headline: The full system.
slides.body: booked-job.com/blog/get-more-google-reviews

slides.headline: Surviving the slow season
slides.body: A cash-flow playbook for the trades.

slides.headline: Win it in the busy season
slides.body: Bank a 3–6 month cushion. Sweep a fixed % of every job before it feels spendable.

slides.headline: Sell weather-proof work
slides.body: Maintenance plans, opposite-season installs, tune-ups — the highest-margin work you'll book.

slides.headline: Mine past customers
slides.body: Your cheapest pipeline isn't a lead site — it's the people who already paid you.

slides.headline: Don't go dark on marketing
slides.body: It's cheapest when competitors go quiet. Pour in then, not at peak prices.

slides.headline: The full playbook.
slides.body: booked-job.com/blog/surviving-the-slow-season
